failure_modes: Bearing Failure due to Mechanical Wear or Dry Running (Cavitation Erosion)
Motor Winding Insulation Breakdown due to Thermal Overload
Electronics Failure due to Voltage Spike or Thermal Overload

criteria.name: Maintenance & Inspection History
criteria.description: Manually assessed score reflecting the recency, completeness, and quality of preventive maintenance and inspection records for this pump.
criteria.ui_label: Maintenance History Risk Score (1=Excellent/Recent PM, 10=No Record/Overdue)

criteria.name: Bearing & Mechanical Condition (Vibration)
criteria.description: Assesses mechanical degradation risk via vibration score, which is a direct indicator of bearing wear, rotor imbalance, and cavitation erosion in centrifugal pumps.

penalties.description: High rotational speed amplifies vibration-induced bearing wear and cavitation erosion risk

criteria.name: Motor Winding Thermal Stress
criteria.description: Evaluates thermal overload risk to motor windings using winding temperature, which is the primary driver of insulation breakdown failures in this pump series.

penalties.description: Elevated current draw accelerates winding heat generation and insulation degradation risk

criteria.name: SPM & Bearing Thermal Condition
criteria.description: Monitors SPM temperature as an indicator of bearing lubrication failure, dry running, or mechanical friction heat buildup within the pump.

penalties.description: Co-occurring elevated vibration with high SPM temperature indicates accelerated bearing failure progression

criteria.name: Power Supply & Electronics Stability
criteria.description: Assesses electronics failure risk by evaluating mains voltage deviations from the nominal operating band, as voltage spikes and instability are a confirmed root cause of electronics failures in this pump.

penalties.description: High current draw under abnormal voltage conditions further stresses electronics components and raises failure risk

criteria.name: Fluid Operating Condition & Cavitation Risk
criteria.description: Evaluates cavitation erosion and dry-running risk based on fluid temperature, where elevated temperatures reduce fluid density and promote cavitation in centrifugal pumps.

penalties.description: High pump speed at elevated fluid temperatures significantly increases cavitation erosion risk and accelerates bearing wear

criteria.name: Operational Criticality & Redundancy
criteria.description: Manually assessed score reflecting the criticality of this pump within the system, the consequence of failure on operations, and the availability of a standby unit.
criteria.ui_label: Asset Criticality Score (1=Low Criticality/Standby Available, 10=Critical/No Redundancy)
